description_long: This house features a traditional timber-framed structure with white plaster infill and dark wooden beams, evoking a classic European medieval or Tudor style. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with reddish-brown, textured tiles, with a main gable intersected by a smaller, perpendicular gable. The building is elevated on wooden posts at one end, creating an overhanging upper story, and the lower level includes a small wooden door and several rectangular windows with wooden shutters. The windows and doors are simply framed, and the overall design is compact, with a slightly asymmetrical layout. The exterior is dominated by earthy tones, with the roof tiles providing a warm contrast to the muted walls and dark wood. The house appears suitable for a residential neighborhood or a historical urban setting.
description: A timber-framed, two-story house with white plaster walls, dark wooden beams, and a steep, reddish-brown tiled gable roof. The design features an overhanging upper story and simple rectangular windows and doors.
description_view_0: Side view showing the steep gable roof, overhanging upper story, and supporting wooden posts beneath the structure.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the smaller gable, a single window, and the intersection of the two roof sections.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the main entry door, two windows, and the prominent timber framing on the facade.
description_view_3: Rear view with a large expanse of roof tiles and a symmetrical arrangement of beams and windows.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door at ground level with no porch, steps, or decorative overhang.
